channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Meta has a new app to let you make little AI experiences
item.description: Meta has launched a new social app called Pocket that lets you make and share little interactive "gizmos" with an AI prompt, as reported by Business Insider. Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is all in on AI as the new social media, and he's previously described a vision of how users could use AI to make […]

item.title: Samsung is plotting to replace your M.2 SSD with a storage chip smaller than a fingernail to improve battery life and supercharge ondevice AI inference
item.description: Faster, smaller and more power efficient

item.title: New Alibaba AI framework skips loading every tool, cutting agent token use 99%
item.description: As enterprise AI systems scale to handle complex workflows, practitioners face the challenge of routing subtasks to the right tools and skills. Agents can have hundreds of tools and skills and get confused on which one to use for each step of a workflow.To address this challenge, researchers at Alibaba developed SkillWeaver, a framework that creates an execution graph for a given task and chooses the right skills for each of the nodes. They also introduce Skill-Aware Decomposition (SAD), a novel technique that uses a feedback loop to enable the agent to fetch and vet relevant tool candidates i...

item.title: How Big Is ‘Love Island USA’? More Than 10 Million People Are Already on Its App
item.description: “We have more people voting on the ‘Love Island USA’ app than we do in many political elections taking place across the country,” says the show's executive producer.

item.title: IQM, Europe’s first public quantum company, admits the future of the tech is uncertain
item.description: IQM, a full-stack quantum company out of Finland, went public on the Nasdaq today at a valuation of about $1.9 billion.

item.title: Gay dating app Goose accused of using AI models to drum up interest
item.description: Gay dating app Goose is accused of using AI-generated models to send promotions to gay men.

item.title: The best July 4th sales we found so far
item.description: July 4th sales are typically a precursor to what we’d see during a mid-July Prime Day, but obviously things are flipped around this year. Last week’s big Prime Day sale is over, yet there are a number of familiar deals still poking around in the week leading up to the nation’s birthday. Best Buy is […]

item.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
item.description: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But low and behold.

item.title: Stellantis renonce au thermique et à l’hybride pour sa future 2CV et ses petites voitures électriques
item.description: Stellantis vient de trancher un débat qu'on croyait acquis depuis des années : pour sa future gamme de petites voitures inspirée de la 2CV, exit l'hybride et le thermique, place au tout-électrique. Une entorse notable à la fameuse "liberté de choix" tant vantée par le groupe.

item.title: How to watch Portugal vs Croatia: Free Streams, TV Channels & Kick-Off time as Cristiano Ronaldo faces Luka Modric at FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Here's how to watch Portugal vs Croatia for free online and from anywhere at the FIFA World Cup 2026, as two greats collide in the last 32.

item.title: Anthropic's Claude to help Micron design better HBM, DRAM, and SSD for AI even as both companies refuse to address computational storage directly
item.description: AI-designed solutions for AI-designated consumption?

item.title: The final season of The Bear gets redemption right
item.description: The show keeps the promise it made since Season 1.

item.title: Sephora just announced a global store change customers have been asking for
item.description: Sephora will reduce music, minimize strong scents and adjust in-store screens during designated quiet hours across its stores globally. Sephora just broke new ground in the beauty industry, becoming the first beauty retailer to launch sensory-friendly shopping hours at a global scale.

item.title: Another mysterious iPhone bug is eating up over 100GB of some people’s storage — here’s how to fix it
item.description: Some iPhone users are losing over 100GB of storage space to ‘System Data’ on their devices.

item.title: A $70 Microsoft Office deal skips the monthly 365 bill
item.description: Get a lifetime license to Microsoft Office Home & Business 2021 — including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook — for $69.97 (reg. $219).

item.title: Maybe robots don't need legs or fingers to do laundry — Weave Robotics Isaac 1 isn't pretty or super-humanoid, but it might be ready to handle basic chores
item.description: Weave Robotics Isaac 1 is a legless robot body on a stalk that can fold laundry and do basic chores, all for $7999.

item.title: What it takes to rebuild America’s competitive edge
item.description: It means rebuilding how capital moves. Inclusive and profitable economic ecosystems can be built, invested in, and scaled.

item.title: Social Security update 2026: The SSA is making a big change starting in July for America’s 250th anniversary. What it means for citizens
item.description: The Social Security Administration revealed this week that will be commemorating American independence in a unique way. The Social Security Administration (SSA) said Wednesday it plans to commemorate the 250th anniversary of American independence on July 4 in a unique way.

item.title: Northern lights tonight: 9 states could see the aurora borealis, thanks to a solar flare. Here’s the forecast for where and when
item.description: Fireworks may not be the only dazzling thing you see this week. Fireworks may not be the only dazzling thing you see this week. There’s a possibility you may get a glimpse of the Northern Lights starting tonight.

item.title: Asus ProArt PZ14 review: This surprisingly powerful tablet PC transformed my creative workflow
item.description: This tablet PC is not just for browsing the internet and editing; it features a full version of Windows and, combined with a powerful AI CPU, offers a complete solution.

item.title: Planning a cookout? There’s one grocery item costing Americans a lot more this year.
item.description: Americans are paying the highest beef prices on record just as millions prepare to fire up the grill for Independence Day Heading into Fourth of July weekend, Americans are going to be making some tough choices about what to throw on the grill.

item.title: The Empire State Building climbing stunt is now brand-trend slop
item.description: The meme to brand-marketing pipeline is shorter (and more boring) than ever. The Empire State Building is one of (if not the most) iconic symbols of New York City, so it’s unsurprising that many people go to extreme lengths to see it. But a recent pair of visitors have taken it too far.

item.title: A warning sign about AI’s real cost, courtesy of Google and Amazon
item.description: AI has made it a lot harder for tech companies like Amazon and Google to deliver on their net-zero pledges.

item.title: Despite the backlash, Donald Trump is using AI again
item.description: Trump’s Truth Social feed is filling with AI-generated posts, including fake celebrities, Teddy Roosevelt, and a golden eagle.

item.title: The enduring power of the boutique model
item.description: Why smaller firms deliver outsized impact. In an era defined by scale, consolidation, and global reach, the prevailing assumption is that bigger is inherently better. Larger firms promise breadth of capability. They also promise deep benches and operational efficiency at scale. Yet, over the last four decades, an alternative model has consistently proven its value. It eschews size for depth, along with continuity, and human connection....
